---
title: "About"
subtitle: "Who writes this log, and why."
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/moiseszeleny
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:your-email@example.com"        # TODO: replace placeholder
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: ORCID
      href: "https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0000" # TODO: replace placeholder
    - icon: file-earmark-text
      text: arXiv
      href: "https://arxiv.org/a/your-arxiv-id"      # TODO: replace placeholder
    - icon: google
      text: Scholar
      href: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=YOUR_ID" # TODO: replace
---

<!-- =========================================================================
     PLACEHOLDER BIO — fill these sections in. Everything below is a draft
     scaffold for you (Moises) to edit. Delete these HTML comments when done.
     ========================================================================= -->

## Physics background

*TODO — a few sentences on who you are as a researcher.* For example: your
position and institution, that you work in **theoretical / particle physics**
with a focus on **Beyond–the–Standard–Model (BSM) phenomenology**, the kinds of
questions you work on (e.g. extended scalar/gauge sectors, neutrino masses, dark
matter, collider or flavour signatures), and the tools you live in day to day
(e.g. `SARAH`, `SPheno`, `FeynRules`, `MadGraph`, `micrOMEGAs`, Mathematica,
Python).

## Why this log exists

*TODO — your motivation in your own words.* A short version to get you started:
I use large language models as part of my research workflow, and I wanted an
honest, reproducible record of **where they genuinely help and where they
don't** — derivations, code, literature triage, model building — without the
marketing gloss. See the [first post](../posts/why-this-log-exists/) for the
longer statement of intent.

## About the posts

Each entry documents a single **real task**: the tool and model used, the
workflow, what worked, what failed, the time saved, and — wherever possible — a
link to the code or notebook so you can reproduce it. Posts are written in
**English or Spanish**, one primary language each, with a short abstract in the
other language at the top.

::: {.callout-note}
Details tied to **unpublished results** are withheld until the corresponding
preprint is public. Reproducibility notes point only to code and material I'm
free to share.
:::

## Contact

- **Email:** *TODO — your-email@example.com*
- **GitHub:** [\@moiseszeleny](https://github.com/moiseszeleny)
- **ORCID / arXiv / Scholar:** *TODO — replace the placeholder links above*
